description_long: This is a large, two-story suburban house with a complex, multi-sectioned layout and a dark gray, shingled hip roof. The exterior walls are clad in beige horizontal siding with white trim around the windows, doors, and roof edges. The front features a covered porch supported by four columns, a central entryway, and symmetrical gables above the main entrance and side wings. The rear of the house includes a bay window extension and multiple window arrangements, while the side view reveals a spacious three-car garage. The windows are rectangular and evenly distributed, with some grouped in pairs or threes, and the overall design is balanced and inviting. The house sits on a low concrete foundation and is typical of modern American suburban neighborhoods.
description: A large, beige two-story suburban house with a dark hip roof, covered porch, and attached three-car garage. Features symmetrical gables, white trim, and a mix of window arrangements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch with four columns, central entryway, symmetrical gables, and main facade details.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the three-car garage, roofline, and minimal window placement on the garage wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the bay window extension, multiple window groupings, and the back entrance area.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the side elevation, roof complexity, and fewer windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with bay window and grouped sets
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with covered porch, four supporting columns, steps, and decorative gables
